title: Macramé letter wall art
subtitle: A single letter filled with square knots and mounted on a wire or dowel
excerpt: Outline a single letter from an alphabet template, wrap it in macramé cord, and fill the interior with square knots to make a wall-art piece about 25 cm tall. Works well as a nursery or bedroom decoration.
sourceNotes: Macramé letter wall art is a widely practiced contemporary craft project in British and international macramé communities. The technique adapts the square-knot fill to irregular shapes using a wire or card letter form.

glossaryTerms.definition: The primary macramé knot: two working cords tied in sequence around two filler cords, alternating the direction of each half. Fills the interior of a letter form with a tightly knotted fabric.
A mounting knot that attaches a folded cord to a support. The folded loop passes over the wire frame or dowel, and both tails pass through the loop and are pulled down.

content.text: A macramé letter is made by wrapping a wire or thick card letter form in cord, then filling the interior with

mounted on the frame cords. Choose a simple bold letter shape; avoid letters with very thin strokes (like I or J) for a first attempt. The result is suitable for any age group when choosing colours that suit the room.

items.name: Wire letter form or thick cardboard letter template (25 cm tall)
Floral wire or picture-hanging wire (for making letter frame if needed)

content.text: Tie the cord end to the letter frame at one corner and wind it tightly around the whole outline, covering the frame with a single layer of cord. Secure the end with a tight knot. This forms the foundation that the fill cords will mount onto.
Cut cords 80 cm long and mount them across the widest horizontal span of the letter interior using
, spacing them 1.5 cm apart. For a 25 cm tall letter, you will need approximately 16 to 20 cords depending on the letter width.

Work horizontal rows of alternating square knots from the top of the mounted cords downward, using 4 cords per group. At the letter edge, tie the last cord to the frame with an overhand knot rather than a square knot. Trim the cord ends to 5 mm and tuck them into the knot structure with a pin.

items.problem: The fill cords are too long and bunch up at the bottom of the letter
items.cause: Cords were cut longer than needed for the letter height
items.fix: Cut cords to about three times the interior letter height. If cords are too long, slide the excess through the last overhand knot before trimming.
